title: The One with All the Candy

jokes.text: No, no. One daddy, two mommies.
jokes.setup: Ben thanks 'Daddy' for helping with his helmet
jokes.punchline: Ross corrects the family structure in a matter-of-fact way
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Ross's awkward navigation of his divorced family situation. Economical and true to character.

jokes.text: It was my sixth birthday. My dad took me to the park... and I got on it and... it bent.
jokes.setup: Ross reminisces about his first bike in a nostalgic way
jokes.punchline: The bike immediately broke under his weight as a child
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expectation with perfect timing. The pause before 'it bent' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: But she gave me the box that it came in. It had a picture of the bike on the front. So I would sit on it and my stepdad would drag me around the backyard.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describes wanting the neighbor girl's bike but never getting to ride it
jokes.punchline: She reveals the sad substitute of riding the empty box
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Phoebe's tragic-comedic childhood while being genuinely touching and funny. The visual is absurd but heartbreaking.

jokes.text: That is so unfair. / Not really, I got to drag him around too.
jokes.setup: Monica expresses sympathy for Phoebe's sad childhood story
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reveals it was actually reciprocal fun
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that recontextualizes the previous story. Classic Phoebe logic delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: the red-haired guy who does not like to be called Rusty
jokes.setup: Monica describes her limited knowledge of neighbors
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific detail about the redhead's name preference
jokes.explanation: Good specificity that suggests an awkward interaction happened off-screen. The precision makes it funny.

jokes.text: Wouldn't it be easier if we just moved?
jokes.setup: Monica wants to get to know the neighbors better
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests the nuclear option instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - practical but antisocial. Shows his character's avoidance tendencies.

jokes.text: We are not... going to let it... be a problem. / Wow. It took you all night to come up with that plan?
jokes.setup: Ross hesitantly reveals their 'master plan' for dating his assistant
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out how pathetically simple it is
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Ross's nervous delivery and Chandler's dry observation about the plan's obviousness.

jokes.text: Matt Wire, Mark Lynn, Ben Wise... / Anymore.
jokes.setup: Rachel lists guys she's slept with on first dates to prove she doesn't
jokes.punchline: She immediately corrects herself
jokes.explanation: Classic Rachel moment of accidentally revealing too much then trying to backtrack. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Are you serious? / No, I've just always wanted to do that.
jokes.setup: Rachel appears to be propositioning Tag professionally
jokes.punchline: She reveals she just wanted to clear papers off desk dramatically
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection with visual payoff. Shows Rachel's playful side while maintaining workplace tension.

jokes.text: Did you eat all the neighbor candy? / Uh, well, yeah, that was the plan. But by the time I got to it there was only a couple pieces left.
jokes.setup: Monica discovers empty candy basket and suspects Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey admits guilt but claims he barely got any
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - admits to the crime while acting like the victim. Shows his food obsession and twisted reasoning.

jokes.text: Make some more lasagna too. Something might have happened to a huge chunk of it.
jokes.setup: Joey asks Monica to make more candy
jokes.punchline: He euphemistically admits to eating the lasagna too
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey food humor with his characteristic passive voice to avoid responsibility.

jokes.text: Mine stole my newspaper. It's like a crime wave.
jokes.setup: Ross responds to Monica's neighbor enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Equates newspaper theft with Monica's candy success as crime wave
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Ross's tendency to dramatize minor inconveniences. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, and Chandler's about to cry. / Am not.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment after Phoebe gets her first bike
jokes.punchline: Phoebe calls out Chandler's emotional response, he denies it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment playing on Chandler's emotional defenses and Phoebe's directness.

jokes.text: Do you know what time it is? / It's candy time.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what time it is (implying it's late)
jokes.punchline: Gary gives literal candy-focused answer
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that establishes the neighbor's single-minded focus on candy.

jokes.text: He does not know that I am not 'some of us.'
jokes.setup: Monica tells neighbor some of us have to work early
jokes.punchline: She reveals to audience she doesn't actually have to work
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic aside that shows Monica's deception while maintaining her authority.

jokes.text: I said that you were a good kisser... and, uh, that I liked your teeny, tiny tushy.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains what she wrote in Tag's fake evaluation
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate personal comments
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor as the professional situation becomes increasingly inappropriate.

jokes.text: Yes, he was able to unhook my bra with minimal supervision.
jokes.setup: Rachel continues describing inappropriate evaluation comments
jokes.punchline: Treats intimate act like job performance metric
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor treating romantic interaction as workplace skill. The 'minimal supervision' detail makes it perfect.

jokes.text: And under problems with performance, I wrote, 'Dear God, I hope not.'
jokes.setup: Rachel continues listing inappropriate evaluation comments
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about performance in different contexts
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre that works on workplace and sexual levels. Well-constructed escalation.

jokes.text: Hey, you cry every time somebody talks about Titanic. / Those two had only each other.
jokes.setup: Ross calls out Chandler for emotional responses
jokes.punchline: Chandler defensively justifies crying over Titanic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's hidden sensitivity and need to justify emotions.

jokes.text: See?
jokes.setup: Friends ask to see Phoebe ride the bike
jokes.punchline: She clearly just walks with it but acts like she demonstrated riding
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy with Phoebe's confidence in clearly not riding the bike. The single word delivery is genius.

jokes.text: Because 'tushy' can mean both ass... and good worker.
jokes.setup: Rachel hopes the evaluation wasn't taken the wrong way
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically suggests alternative meaning
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm pointing out the obvious impossibility of Rachel's hope.

jokes.text: I tell you, this little talent came in handy before I could afford porn.
jokes.setup: Joey shows off his pornographic drawing skills
jokes.punchline: Reveals personal history with characteristic oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey oversharing that reveals too much personal information in his matter-of-fact way.

jokes.text: Maybe. Isn't she the woman who lives below you and has sex really loud? / BOTH: Yeah, that's her.
jokes.setup: Chandler identifies neighbor by sexual sounds
jokes.punchline: Both immediately recognize her by this description
jokes.explanation: Good recognition humor with the immediate dual confirmation adding to the comedy.

jokes.text: You know what would work? Stop making candy.
jokes.setup: Monica can't believe her 'no candy after hours' sign didn't work
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests the obvious solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler practical solution that cuts through Monica's complexity.

jokes.text: You want to talk about getting people to like you, huh, funny man?
jokes.setup: Monica suggests she cooks to get people to like her
jokes.punchline: She calls out Chandler's comedy as serving the same purpose
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on Chandler's character while advancing their relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: Perhaps like an airplane seat, or a beanbag chair.
jokes.setup: Phoebe complains about uncomfortable bike seat
jokes.punchline: Suggests completely impractical alternatives
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurdist thinking with specific, impossible alternatives.

jokes.text: Okay. I would ring the bell to distract him... and then I would knock the gun out of his hand with a Chinese throwing star.
jokes.setup: Ross creates elaborate bike-riding emergency scenario
jokes.punchline: Phoebe has even more elaborate non-bike solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation that's perfectly Phoebe. The specific details make it funnier.

jokes.text: You swore.
jokes.setup: Ross lets go of the bike after promising he wouldn't
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's simple, hurt accusation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of words capturing the betrayal and hurt simply.

jokes.text: By the way, the week before your wedding, you may not see a lot of me.
jokes.setup: Monica is obsessively making candy
jokes.punchline: Warns about pre-wedding obsessive behavior
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Monica's self-awareness about her obsessive tendencies.

jokes.text: Well, I would love to... but the bike got stolen and the police have no suspects.
jokes.setup: Ross asks Phoebe to try riding the bike again
jokes.punchline: Phoebe lies about bike being stolen
jokes.explanation: Decent lie with the unnecessary detail about 'no suspects' making it more elaborate.

jokes.text: The bike... is dying.
jokes.setup: Ross argues the bike wants to be ridden
jokes.punchline: Dramatically declares the bike is dying from neglect
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with dramatic delivery making the ridiculous concept funnier.

jokes.text: All right. If you care enough to make up that load of crap, okay.
jokes.setup: Ross gives elaborate speech about the bike's feelings
jokes.punchline: Phoebe sees right through it but agrees anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe response calling out Ross's nonsense while being touched by his effort.

jokes.text: You wrote that you have a cute tushy? / Yes. I have a weird sense of humor. And I'm kind of strangely proud of my butt.
jokes.setup: Tag takes blame for inappropriate evaluation
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly explains writing about his own butt
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Tag has to defend writing about his own attractiveness.

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? With a cute butt like this, I'd find work.
jokes.setup: Rachel worries Tag could have lost his job
jokes.punchline: Tag confidently references his earlier butt comment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Tag's confidence and plays off the established butt joke.

jokes.text: Candy Lady? / No, not 'Candy Lady.' / If we know it, can we get candy?
jokes.setup: Joey asks if neighbors know Monica's name
jokes.punchline: They only know her as Candy Lady and just want candy
jokes.explanation: Shows the neighbors' single-minded focus on candy over actual relationship.

jokes.text: No, smokes-a-lot lady blew smoke directly into my mouth.
jokes.setup: Asked if she smoked
jokes.punchline: References earlier neighbor description with specific detail
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier neighbor description with visual comedy implied.

jokes.text: Don't be so corny, Ross. It's not an After School Special.
jokes.setup: Ross gives inspirational speech about overcoming fears
jokes.punchline: Phoebe calls out the cheesy life lesson format
jokes.explanation: Good meta-comedy acknowledging the sitcom's tendency toward cheesy moral moments.

tmdb_overview: Ross buys Phoebe a bike after learning she never had one, but she can't ride it. Monica makes candy for neighbors who ignore her. Rachel and Tag's relationship causes HR issues.
